rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in the target component implementation scope after subcomponents and connections
Preserve existing AADL architecture by inserting annex blocks without modifying component structure
Use only visible identifiers from the current scope or explicitly declared constants
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through variables or outputs
generic_rules: Maintain architectural integrity by only inserting annex blocks without altering component structure
Use only visible identifiers from the current scope or explicitly declared constants
Preserve existing connections and subcomponents when inserting annex blocks
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through variables or outputs
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use `eq name : type = expression;` for equation declarations
Use `const name : type = value;` for constant declarations
Use `assign output = expression;` for implementation-level bindings
anti_patterns: Do not use AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions without explicit typed ports or constants
Do not use subcomponent paths in component type contracts
Do not modify the existing AADL architecture when inserting annex blocks
Do not use identifiers not visible in the current scope without explicit declaration
